key_insight: STRAND reframes persistence diagrams as survival data, enabling a single interpretable representation for hypothesis testing, effect sizes, and stable vectorization.
engineering_relevance: It gives practitioners a calibrated statistical test and a machine-learning-ready feature map for topological data analysis in graph, point-cloud, and neuroscience pipelines.
method: Treat each persistence value p=d-b as a time-to-event and model the persistence survival function S(t)=P(p>t).
Derive a non-parametric two-sample test with calibrated Type I error and interpretable effect sizes.
Construct a 1-Wasserstein-stable vectorization for downstream prediction tasks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on synthetic manifolds, 14 graph benchmarks, 3 point-cloud benchmarks, and an fMRI functional connectivity application, showing calibration, power, and competitive performance.
watch_item: The approach depends on treating persistence values as survival observations, so results may be sensitive to how diagrams are constructed, filtered, and sampled across datasets.
paper_title: From Persistence to Survival: Hypothesis Testing, Effect Sizes and Vectorisation for Topological Features
